methods.name: odoo.addons.account_reconcile_model_oca.models.account_reconcile_model.AccountReconcileModel._apply_lines_for_bank_widget
documentation:
Apply the reconciliation model lines to the statement line passed as
parameter.
\:param residual_amount_currency: The open balance of the statement line in the

> bank reconciliation widget expressed in the statement line currency.

```
param partner

The partner set on the wizard.

param st_line

The statement line processed by the bank reconciliation widget.

return

A list of python dictionaries (one per reconcile model line)
representing the journal items to be created by the current reconcile model.
```
RECONCILIATION PROCESS

methods.name: odoo.addons.account_reconcile_model_oca.models.account_reconcile_model.AccountReconcileModel._apply_rules
documentation:
Apply criteria to get candidates for all reconciliation models.
This function is called in enterprise by the reconciliation widget to match
the statement line with the available candidates (using the reconciliation
models).
\:param st_line: The statement line to match.
\:param partner: The partner to consider.
\:return: A dict mapping each statement line id with:

> - aml_ids: A list of account.move.line ids.
> - model: An account.reconcile.model record (optional).
> - status: 'reconciled' if the lines has been already reconciled, 'write_off'
>   if the write-off must be applied on the statement line.
> - auto_reconcile: A flag indicating if the match is enough significant to
>   auto reconcile the candidates.
RECONCILIATION CRITERIA

methods.name: odoo.addons.account_reconcile_model_oca.models.account_reconcile_model.AccountReconcileModel._is_applicable_for
documentation:
Returns true iff this reconciliation model can be used to search for matches
for the provided statement line and partner.

methods.name: odoo.addons.account_reconcile_model_oca.models.account_reconcile_model.AccountReconcileModel._get_invoice_matching_st_line_tokens
documentation:
Parse the textual information from the statement line passed as parameter
in order to extract from it the meaningful information in order to perform the
matching.
\:param st_line: A statement line.
\:return: A list of tokens, each one being a string.

methods.name: odoo.addons.account_reconcile_model_oca.models.account_reconcile_model.AccountReconcileModel._get_invoice_matching_amls_candidates
documentation:
Returns the match candidates for the 'invoice_matching' rule, with respect to
the provided parameters.
\:param st_line: A statement line.
\:param partner: The partner associated to the statement line.

methods.name: odoo.addons.account_reconcile_model_oca.models.account_reconcile_model.AccountReconcileModel._get_invoice_matching_rules_map
documentation:
Get a mapping \<priority_order, rule> that could be overridden in others
modules.
\:return: a mapping \<priority_order, rule> where:

> - priority_order: Defines in which order the rules will be evaluated, the
>   lowest comes first. This is extremely important since the algorithm stops
>   when a rule returns some candidates.
> - rule: Method taking \<st_line, partner> as parameters and returning the
>   candidates journal items found.

methods.name: odoo.addons.account_reconcile_model_oca.models.account_reconcile_model.AccountReconcileModel._get_partner_from_mapping
documentation:
Find partner with mapping defined on model.
For invoice matching rules, matches the statement line against each
regex defined in partner mapping, and returns the partner corresponding
to the first one matching.
:param st_line (Model<account.bank.statement.line>):
    The statement line that needs a partner to be found
:return Model<res.partner>:
    The partner found from the mapping. Can be empty an empty recordset
    if there was nothing found from the mapping or if the function is
    not applicable.

methods.name: odoo.addons.account_reconcile_model_oca.models.account_reconcile_model.AccountReconcileModel._check_rule_propositions
documentation:
Check restrictions that can't be handled for each move.line separately.
Note: Only used by models having a type equals to 'invoice_matching'.
\:param st_line: The statement line.
\:param amls_values_list: The candidates account.move.line as a list of dict:

> - aml: The record.
> - amount_residual: The amount residual to consider.
> - amount_residual_currency: The amount residual in foreign currency to
>   consider.

```
return

A string representing what to do with the candidates:
* rejected: Reject candidates.
* allow_write_off: Allow to generate the write-off from the reconcile model

source:12: (ERROR/3) Unexpected indentation.

lines if specified.

source:13: (WARNING/2) Block quote ends without a blank line; unexpected unindent.

allow_auto_reconcile: Allow to automatically reconcile entries if
'auto_validate' is enabled.
```

methods.name: odoo.addons.account_reconcile_model_oca.tests.test_reconciliation_match.TestReconciliationMatchingRules.test_invoice_matching_rule_no_partner
documentation:
Tests that a statement line without any partner can be matched to the
right invoice if they have the same payment reference.

methods.name: odoo.addons.account_reconcile_model_oca.tests.test_reconciliation_match.TestReconciliationMatchingRules.test_match_multi_currencies
documentation:
Ensure the matching of candidates is made using the right statement line
currency. In this test, the value of the statement line is 100 USD = 300
GOL = 900 DAR and we want to match two journal items of:
- 100 USD = 200 GOL (= 600 DAR from the statement line point of view)
- 14 USD = 280 DAR
Both journal items should be suggested to the user because they represents 98%
of the statement line amount (DAR).

methods.name: odoo.addons.account_reconcile_model_oca.tests.test_reconciliation_match.TestReconciliationMatchingRules.test_no_amount_check_keep_first
documentation:
In case the reconciliation model doesn't check the total amount of the
candidates, we still don't want to suggest more than are necessary to match the
statement. For example, if a statement line amounts to 250 and is to be matched
with three invoices of 100, 200 and 300 (retrieved in this order), only 100 and
200 should be proposed.

methods.name: odoo.addons.account_reconcile_model_oca.tests.test_reconciliation_match.TestReconciliationMatchingRules.test_no_amount_check_exact_match
documentation:
If a reconciliation model finds enough candidates for a full reconciliation,
it should still check the following candidates, in case one of them exactly
matches the amount of the statement line. If such a candidate exist, all the
other ones are disregarded.
